quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is named after a Greek sailor who sailed under a Spanish name! His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but he explored the Pacific Northwest in 1592 as Juan de Fuca. The waterway you can see from Port Angeles has connected cultures and explorers for centuries.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, like the ones you might spot in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, can hold their breath for nearly 30 minutes and dive over 1,500 feet deep. Learn about these charming marine neighbors and their surprising abilities.

medium_read.title: The Return of Wildflowers to Olympic National Park
medium_read.teaser: Spring brings an explosion of color to the Olympic Peninsula's meadows, from avalanche lilies to lupines. Discover which flowers are blooming right now and where to find the best displays near Port Angeles.

long_read.title: The Remarkable Restoration of the Elwha River
long_read.teaser: After the largest dam removal project in U.S. history, the Elwha River is returning to life with salmon swimming freely for the first time in a century. This is the inspiring story of how nature heals when given the chance.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Your Backyard
port_angeles.description: The Olympic Peninsula is home to the Columbian black-tailed deer, a subspecies found only in the Pacific Northwest. These gentle creatures are often spotted around Port Angeles at dawn and dusk, especially near wooded areas. This time of year, you might even see adorable spotted fawns following their mothers!

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart is working incredibly hard to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are amazing — they beat about 1,200 times per minute during flight! That's what powers their incredible hovering ability.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals, which is why they need to eat every 10-15 minutes and consume up to half their body weight in sugar daily. When they sleep, their heart rate drops to as low as 50 beats per minute to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's absolutely right — and such a beautiful word to say!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'köszönöm' (or the shorter 'köszi' among friends). 'Szia' means hello, 'igen' means yes, and 'viszlát' means goodbye!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European languages that surround it. It's known for its beautiful sound and the way it builds words by adding suffixes — one word can express what takes a whole sentence in English!

puzzle_3.question: What is the tallest tree species in the world?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Coast redwoods can grow over 380 feet tall — and some of their cousins grow right here in Olympic National Park!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the coast redwood! While the Olympic Peninsula has magnificent Sitka spruce and Douglas fir trees, coast redwoods in California hold the record at over 380 feet tall.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The tallest known tree in the world is a coast redwood named Hyperion, standing at 380.3 feet — taller than the Statue of Liberty! Olympic National Park has some of the largest Sitka spruce trees in the world, which can reach nearly 300 feet tall.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
